title: Alkaline-SDS cell lysis of microbes with acetone protein precipitation for proteomic sample preparation in 96-well plate format
author: Yan Chen

steps_raw.instruction: Start with 10 µL of cells per well a non-skirted PCR plate (Thermo Scientific, Cat.#AB0600). Plate of cell pellets When processing larger amounts of biomass increase reagent volumes accordingly. This protocol works in larger formats (e.g., 1.7 mL Eppendorf tubes) too.
Resuspend the cell pellet in lysis buffer on a plate mixer. Ensure proper mixing of the cell lysis buffer and the cell pellet. Plate mixer Cells resuspended in lysis buffer
Add 2.75 µL 1 Molarity (M), Hydrochloric acid (Sigma Cat.#320331) to each well.
Add 25 µL, 100 mM Ammonium bicarbonate (VWR Scientific Cat.#BJ40867-50G) and 0.5 µL, Benzonase nuclease (Millipore Cat.#70746) to each well.
Mix thoroughly on the plate mixer. Cell lysate after benzonase treatment
Add 200 µL, 100% Acetone (Sigma Cat.#179124) to each well and let sit at room temperature for 5 minutes. Protein precipitation after mixing in 100% acetone
Add 60 µL of 100 millimolar (mM), Ammonium bicarbonate in 10% Methanol to each well to resuspend protein pellet. Protein pellet resuspended in 60 uL of Ammonium bicarbonate in 10% Methanol
Remove supernatant. Protein pellet after centrifugation and removal of supernatant
Wash protein pellet twice using 150 µL, 80% Acetone (Sigma Cat.#179124) . Increase the number of washes depending on the sample matrix background.
Store at -20 °C until ready for Automated Protein Quantitation with the Biomek-FX liquid handler system .
Add 25 µL of alkaline-SDS cell lysis buffer (200 mM NaOH, 1% SDS) to each well.
